definition: A standard of judicial respect for agency interpretations of statutes. Under Skidmore v. Swift & Co., courts give weight to an agency's interpretation based on its thoroughness, reasoning, consistency, and other persuasive qualities — less deferential than the now-overruled Chevron deference.
examples: West Virginia argued that the longstanding HEW athletics regulations deserve substantial weight under Skidmore because they are contemporaneous with the statute and have been continuously applied.
